handoff.fern_179: λ-robust topology contingency: the both-bugs recovery bar is λ_bar(LCB)=0.9052 for #83 (central 0.8384≈0.838). Across 182 feasible 32-node max-branch-3 trees the bar is BOUNDED BELOW by 0.8345 (depth-14); at the as-built depth-9 horizon #83 is already the λ-robust optimum. No topology clears 500 at the realistic λ̂=0.342 — the recovery gap (2.4× λ̂) is too large for the topology lever to close. Front-loading is REFUTED; depth (more E[T]@λ=1) is the only lever and it is the same max-E[T] lever, not a robustness-specific one.
handoff.land_71: Contingency map for the verify-tree build: keep #83 (depth-9, max-branch-3) — it is the min-λ_bar topology within the measured recovery horizon. A depth-11 tree lowers LCB λ_bar to 0.8512 ONLY if self-KV recovery is trusted past the depth-7 measurement. Two-level N_eff caveat: under worst-case within-prompt clustering (ICC=1, N_eff=128) the recommended LCB clears-500 = False (iid #175 ±10.9 → worst-case ±54.9). Topology cannot rescue the λ̂=0.342 realistic floor; only the self-KV recovery fix can.
